name: hydrogen_pipeline_leak_model_evacuation_corridor
premise: County emergency manager Casey Chen must set evacuation radius after a hydrogen pipeline pressure anomaly when dispersion models disagree between vendor software and fire department plume calculators by two kilometers. Plant manager Erin Walker argues wider evacuations strand a regional hospital inside the ring during cardiac surge. Narrow rings preserve hospital access but leave subdivisions in a disputed gray zone. Lightning threatens ignition if crews wait.

agents.name: Casey Chen
agents.identity: I am the county emergency manager coordinating HAZMAT mutual aid, school buses, and hospital access during industrial gas incidents.
agents.goal: I want life-first evacuations without trapping critical care inside unevacuable rings during active surgery blocks.

agents.name: Erin Walker
agents.identity: I am the hydrogen plant manager with on-scene telemetry who believes vendor dispersion overstates plume reach in stable air tonight.
agents.goal: I want the narrower scientifically defensible ring to keep arterial routes open for ambulances, accepting personal liability if the wider model was right.

choices: I will order the wider two-kilometer differential ring and medevac critical patients before shifting buses, accepting surgical disruption and helicopter noise complaints.
I will order the narrower vendor ring with shelter-in-place for gray-zone subdivisions and continuous handheld sensor walks, accepting residual exposure if handhelds lag plume edges.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Lightning countdown is fifteen minutes on the incident command screen. Casey Chen must choose between the wider differential evacuation ring or the narrower ring with gray-zone shelter-in-place. What does Casey Chen do?
